channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump vows a 'new chapter' for America, promises the 'best days are yet to come' in pre-Inauguration video
item.description: President-elect Trump says his second term represents a “new chapter" for America and promised the American people that the country’s “best days are yet to come.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden pardons Mark Milley, Anthony Fauci, J6 committee members
item.description: President Biden issued pre-emptive pardons for Dr. Anthony Fauci and others who may have faced scrutiny under the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is an executive order? A look at Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump is returning to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order. An incoming president signing a flurry of executive orders is standard practice.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ recommends 20-year sentence for Pennsylvania man convicted for role in Jan. 6 Capitol riot
item.description: The Department of Justice has proposed that a Pennsylvania barber convicted on multiple charges involving the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot spend 20 years behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colleges, educational groups offer advice for Inauguration Day 'anxiety,' trips to DC for Trump swearing-in
item.description: Colleges and educational groups across the country are gearing up for President-elect Trump's Inauguration Day by offering students mental health advice or a trip to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Law enforcement on high alert for Trump inauguration after 2024 assassination attempts
item.description: Federal, state, and local law enforcement officials will be on high alert on Jan. 20 when President-elect Donald Trump gets sworn into office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York Gov. Hochul orders flags to be raised for Trump's inauguration amid mourning period for Jimmy Carter
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul joined a trio of Democrat governors and a slew of Republican governors in ordering flags to fly at full-staff for the inauguration of President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moving presidents in, out 'mass bedlam': Former White House usher
item.description: Emery said the process of moving presidents in and out of the White House “is almost like Cirque du Soleil.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmaker pushes bill to rename Nashville International Airport after Donald Trump
item.description: Tennessee Republican State Rep. Todd Warner wants to rename Nashville International Airport to Trump International Airport. If this bill is passed, it will take effect on Jan. 1, 2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Melania meme coin launches following Trump's new cryptocurrency
item.description: The website for the coin describes “Melania Memes” as “fungible crypto assets created and tracked on the Solana blockchain.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Raucous MAGA rally punctuates events celebrating Trump on the eve of his inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is using one last rally on the eve of his inauguration to again celebrate his election victory. He declared Sunday, “We won” to a crowd celebrating his return to the White House and projecting defiant optimism despite deep national political divisions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Rubio will meet with peers from India, Japan, Australia after expected confirmation, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration would begin formal international engagements this week when Marco Rubio — expected to be confirmed soon as secretary of state — meets with the foreign ministers of India, Japan and Australia, U.S. officials say.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to take more than 200 executive actions on day one
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: President Trump will sign more than 200 executive actions on his first day in office—a massive, record-setting first wave of policy priorities focused on border security, energy, reducing the cost of living for American families, ending DEI programs across the federal government and more,
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's inauguration will be first attended by foreign leaders
item.description: For the first time in U.S. history, a president-elect will welcome foreign leaders for one of the most American political traditions — the peaceful transfer of power.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to watch, stream Trump's 2025 inauguration on January 20th
item.description: Live broadcasts and special coverage on Inauguration Day 2025 will be streamed on FOX News Channel and FoxNews.com. President-elect Donald Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance will both be sworn into office on the day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden spends his final full day in office in South Carolina. It helped him become president
item.description: Joe Biden is spending his final full day in office Sunday in South Carolina, a state that holds special meaning after his commanding win in the 2020 Democratic primary there set him up to achieve his life's goal of being elected president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump wants to visit China, strengthen relationship: Report
item.description: The president-elect has vowed to impose tariffs and other mechanisms against China upon his return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: What to watch at Trump's inauguration: Big Tech CEOs, Carrie Underwood and foreign leaders
item.description: Donald Trump's inauguration as the country’s 47th president was expected to be an extravagant break from tradition — before it got moved indoors due to cold weather. There will still be well-known performers, influential billionaires as guests and foreign heads of state.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Then and now: What has changed as Trump returns to the White House
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When Donald Trump arrived in Washington in 2017 just before his first inauguration, he was a stranger to most in town. As the only president in history without prior experience in public office or the military, it was unclear how he would govern. Not this time.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clothes in the closets and favorite foods in the fridge: Moving day comes to the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most presidents get to move into the White House once. President-elect Donald Trump is doing it twice, and his wife, Melania, says it's a lot easier the second time around. “I know where I will be going. I know the rooms where we will be living.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration, a triumphant return for the Republican four years after he departed the city under the shadow of an attack by his supporters on the Capit
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marco Rubio met with UAP whistleblower prior to Trump nomination
item.description: Barber said he’s worked in both an official and unofficial capacity for the U.S. government and partners in the intelligence community.
item.dc:creator: Ross Coulthart, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands march in anti-Trump protests just days before former president returns to power
item.description: Demonstrators rallied in St. Paul on Saturday afternoon, braving single-digit temperatures to protest ahead of President-elect Donald Trump inauguration. Trump takes the oath of office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Feven Gerezgiher and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Thousands march in Washington days before Trump takes office
item.description: The march is just one of several protests, rallies and vigils focused on abortion, rights, immigration rights and the Israel-Hamas war planned in advance of inauguration Monday. Around the country, over 350 similar marches are taking place in every state.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, ASHRAF KHALIL and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons: Here’s what to know about potential clemencies
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to make good on his promises to pardon at least some Jan. 6 rioters on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Mass deportation raids could start in Chicago as soon as Tuesday: Report
item.description: Mass deportation raids threatened in Chicago could start as soon as Tuesday, according to a report from the Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic state AGs hurry to preserve Biden gun control
item.description: Trump, in a previous campaign speech, said he would "roll back every Biden attack on the Second Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Free upgrades and pardon requests: What it's like to share a name with a president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There’s not only one Donald Trump in the United States. Nor is there only one William J. Clinton. Plenty of people share names with famous presidents. Donald Trump, a veteran and resident of Alabama, said because of his name he often is gifted things for free. William J.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate advances Laken Riley Act, teeing up final vote
item.description: The Senate voted Friday to advance the Laken Riley Act, putting Republicans a step closer to sending the first bill of the 119th Congress to President-elect Trump for his signature next week and giving him an early win on one of his key issues.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Renew your driver’s license from a browser? New Walz budget seeks online option
item.description: In his newly proposed budget, Gov. Tim Walz suggests allowing online renewal for driver’s licenses. Walz said the move would increase filing fee revenue, while making it more convenient for drivers.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Rudy Giuliani’s Florida condo, World Series rings on the line in latest trial
item.description: Rudy Giuliani will again square off in court against two Georgia election workers Thursday, this time seeking to keep his Florida condominium and other prized possessions from the women who won a $146 million defamation judgment against him.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, PIX 11

item.title: Minnesota courts may get final say in government's constitutional clash
item.description: An ugly opening to Minnesota’s legislative session and a campaign season that won’t end is fueling lawsuit after lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Local reactions mixed as Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal moves forward
item.description: The Middle East and the world are celebrating the Israel-Hamas ceasefire as a possible step toward peace, but many are still cautious about the deal.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC mayor: More beds for mentally ill people, more cops on subways
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- With more NYPD officers heading into the subway system during the overnight hours, New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he is also looking to get mentally ill homeless people off the trains with a new option.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Rubio vows to place US interests 'above all else' as Trump's top diplomat
item.description: Florida Sen. Marco Rubio promised on Wednesday to implement President-elect Donald Trump’s “America First” vision as secretary of state, vowing in his confirmation hearing that the incoming administration will forge a new path by placing American interests “above all else.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATTHEW LEE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
